title: Source Registration, Subscriptions, Following, and Ownership · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Ownership Authorization
summary: Each user creates duplicate fetch jobs for the same feed, unfollow deletes shared data, ownership is ambiguous, and source verification happens outside a transaction. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go ownership authorization increment in shared-source and tenant subscription model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Authorize source administration, subscription access, and moderation by tenant role and resource relationship in repositories.
Model pending, active, paused, moved, failing, disabled, deleted, and appeal states with allowed transitions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: feed-go-ownership-authorization-1tm8ka(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_feed_go_ownership_authorization_1tm8ka\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?registrations < 5)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?sources < 1 \|\| sources >= registrations)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?subscriptions < sources)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?crossTenantLinks > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using authorize and source plus specific case evidence.
